task: Phase 1 backend observability UI API contracts and routes
acceptance_criteria: Technical observability UI has stable backend endpoints for summary aggregates event rows event detail and calibration readiness, backed by decision_observability runtime contracts
validation_command: uv run --project finance-agent-core python -m pytest finance-agent-core/tests -q && uv run --project finance-agent-core python -m ruff check finance-agent-core/api/server.py finance-agent-core/src/agents/technical/subdomains/decision_observability
notes: Focused backend tests, changed-path ruff, contract regeneration, and frontend typecheck passed. Post-review remediation added event detail, widened filter coverage to align with MonitoringQueryScope, and tightened facade usage.

task: Phase 2 frontend route shell navigation and shared filter workspace
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated `/technical-observability` route exists with navigation entry shared filter state and typed client fetch hooks, without polluting the main `/` workspace
validation_command: cd frontend && npm run test && npm run typecheck && npm run lint
notes: Implemented dedicated route shell, shared filter workspace, parser-backed observability hook, and reused primary navigation. Full frontend tests and typecheck passed; changed-path eslint passed.

task: Phase 3 overview and event explorer views
acceptance_criteria: Internal users can inspect headline monitoring metrics unresolved backlog event lists and event detail drill-down from the new page
validation_command: cd frontend && npm run test && npm run typecheck && npm run lint
notes: Implemented explicit overview and event explorer tab components, including backlog summary, event investigation drill-down, and explicit loading/empty/degraded states. Full frontend tests, frontend typecheck, and changed-path eslint passed.

task: Phase 4 cohort analysis and calibration readiness views
acceptance_criteria: Internal users can analyze cohorts by timeframe horizon logic_version and inspect calibration readiness using the new observability page while keeping raw and approved label lenses distinct
validation_command: cd frontend && npm run test && npm run typecheck && npm run lint
notes: Implemented cohort analysis and calibration readiness views with explicit raw-vs-approved lens controls. Full frontend tests, frontend typecheck, and changed-path eslint passed.
